diskripr
  • API Reference

  • View page source

Pipeline

Pipeline orchestrator for the diskripr workflow.

The primary interface is the Pipeline class, which is instantiated with a Config and exposes each stage as a method. Stage results are stored as public instance attributes so the caller can read intermediate state or inject values between stages (e.g. a pre-built Selection from an interactive CLI prompt):

pipeline = Pipeline(config)
pipeline.discover()            # sets pipeline.disc_info

# For ask-mode: build selection interactively from pipeline.disc_info.titles
# and assign it before calling rip:
pipeline.selection = my_selection

pipeline.rip()                 # sets pipeline.rip_results
pipeline.encode()              # sets pipeline.encode_results
pipeline.organize()            # sets pipeline.output_paths

Or run the full chain at once:

output_paths = Pipeline(config).run()

Stage state attributes:

  • disc_info — set by discover()

  • selection — set by run(), or injected externally

  • rip_results — set by rip()

  • encode_results — set by encode()

  • output_paths — set by organize()

The pipeline only handles resolved rip_mode values ("main" or "all") and encode_format values ("h264", "h265", or "none"). The CLI is responsible for resolving "ask" interactively and assigning the resulting Selection to pipeline.selection before calling rip().

Error propagation: if a title fails to rip it is excluded from subsequent stages with a warning rather than a crash. Stage-level exceptions surface as typed errors (RipError, EncodeError) so the caller can handle them distinctly.

class diskripr.pipeline.Pipeline(config: Config)[source]

Bases: object

Stateful orchestrator for the diskripr DVD ripping workflow.

Instantiate with a Config, then call stage methods in order. Each method stores its output on the instance so subsequent stages can access it without passing arguments between calls.

Stages:

  1. discover() — detect the drive and scan titles.

  2. (selection) — built by run(), or set externally on Pipeline.selection for ask-mode CLI workflows.

  3. rip() — extract titles to the temp directory.

  4. encode() — optional re-encoding with HandBrakeCLI.

  5. organize() — move files into the Jellyfin tree.

Use run() to execute all stages in one call.

discover(on_progress: ProgressCallback | None = None) → DiscInfo[source]

Detect the disc drive and scan all available titles.

Steps:

  1. Verify the configured block device exists.

  2. Run lsdvd for a quick disc title pre-check (non-fatal).

  3. Scan drives with MakeMKV; match the configured device path to a drive index. Falls back to drive index 0 if no match is found.

  4. Scan all titles on the matched drive.

  5. Filter titles shorter than config.min_length.

  6. Fail with a diagnostic message if no titles remain.

Sets Pipeline.disc_info on the instance.

Parameters:

on_progress – Reserved for future use; not used by this stage.

Returns:

The populated DiscInfo.

Raises:

RuntimeError – Device not found, no accessible MakeMKV drive, or no titles remain after min_length filtering.

rip(on_progress: ProgressCallback | None = None) → list[RipResult][source]

Extract selected titles from disc to the temp directory.

Requires Pipeline.disc_info and Pipeline.selection to be set (call discover() first and ensure a Selection has been assigned).

Calls makemkvcon mkv for the main title and each classified extra. Per-title RipError failures are logged as warnings; remaining titles continue processing.

Sets Pipeline.rip_results on the instance.

Parameters:

on_progress – Optional progress callback forwarded to the driver.

Returns:

List of RipResult, one per attempted title.

Raises:

RuntimeError – Pipeline.disc_info or Pipeline.selection is not set.

encode(on_progress: ProgressCallback | None = None) → list[EncodeResult][source]

Re-encode ripped titles with HandBrakeCLI (optional stage).

Skipped entirely when config.encode_format is "none" or when HandBrakeCLI is not installed. Per-title EncodeError failures keep the original MKV for that title and log a warning.

When config.keep_original is set, the original pre-encode MKV is moved to an originals/ subdirectory of the temp dir after a successful encode, so organize() can relocate it.

Sets Pipeline.encode_results on the instance.

Parameters:

on_progress – Optional progress callback forwarded to the driver.

Returns:

List of EncodeResult. An empty list signals that organize() should use Pipeline.rip_results.

organize() → list[Path][source]

Move ripped or encoded files into the Jellyfin directory structure.

Builds the Jellyfin tree under config.output_dir, resolves which results to use (encoded if available, ripped otherwise, with per-title fallback when encoding partially failed), moves the main feature and each classified extra, then cleans up the temp directory and optionally ejects the disc.

For single-disc movies, logs a warning when the movie folder already contains MKV files. For multi-disc movies (config.disc_number is set), an existing folder is expected and no warning is emitted.

When config.keep_original is set, pre-encode originals stored in <temp_dir>/originals/ are relocated to <movie_dir>/originals/.

Sets Pipeline.output_paths on the instance.

Returns:

List of pathlib.Path for each file placed in the Jellyfin library tree.

Raises:

RuntimeError – Pipeline.selection is not set.

run(on_progress: ProgressCallback | None = None) → list[Path][source]

Run the full pipeline from disc discovery to Jellyfin organization.

Chains all stages: discover → select → classify → rip → encode → organize, followed by a non-fatal stream inspection pass.

Only handles resolved rip_mode values ("main" or "all") and encode_format values ("h264", "h265", or "none"). The CLI must resolve "ask" interactively and assign the resulting Selection to Pipeline.selection before calling rip() if ask-mode is needed.

Parameters:

on_progress – Optional progress callback for long-running stages.

Returns:

List of pathlib.Path for every file placed in the Jellyfin library tree.

Raises:

RuntimeError – Device not found, no accessible drive, or no titles survive min_length filtering.
